title: dk opam lock 1.0
description: A reproducible, slot-aware lock of a solved opam dependency closure. Generated at author time by CommonsLang_OCaml.Dk.OpamLock@1.0.0 and consumed at build time by a per-package dk build rule. Package metadata is shared across slots and keyed by '<name>.<version>'; each slot records only which package keys are in its solution (opam filters can differ per slot, e.g. conf-mingw only on Windows).

generated.description: Non-authoritative provenance for humans; never read by the build.

opam_repositories.description: Ordered opam repositories (highest priority first) with pinned commits for reproducibility.

packages.description: Map from '<name>.<version>' package key to its locked metadata. Shared by every slot that lists the key in its solution.

slots.description: Map from dk slot name (e.g. 'Release.Linux_x86_64') to the solution solved for that slot.

commit.description: Pinned VCS commit for reproducibility; null for a floating or non-VCS repository.

source.description: Upstream source archive. null for a locally-pinned package, which is built from a project-provided source asset rather than a downloaded archive.

local.description: true when the package is pinned to a project-local source (source is then null).

depends.description: Direct dependency package NAMES that also appear in at least one slot solution. These are the DAG edges the build walks; version-constraint and filter fidelity is preserved in depends_raw.

depends_raw.description: Verbatim opam 'depends:' field, for filter and version-constraint fidelity.

build.description: Verbatim opam 'build:' field in opam command-template format (quoted literals, bare variables such as name/jobs, and {filters}). Resolved per-slot at build time; null when absent.

install.description: Verbatim opam 'install:' field, same format as build; null when absent.

extra.description: Reserved for extra-files, patches, and substs captured in a later revision.

opam_vars.description: The OPAMVAR_* overrides used to solve this slot (e.g. os, arch).

solution.description: The package keys ('<name>.<version>') forming this slot's closure. Order is not significant; the build topologically orders via each package's depends edges.
